titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Eileen Myles Papers
titlestmt.author: by Nora Soto

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 6 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 8 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday May 27, 2025 at 23:03

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains writings, correspondence, notebooks and journals, personal and professional papers, printed material, computer and audiovisual media, and other papers by or relating to the writer, poet, and performance artist Eileen Myles.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 61 (student records and other material): Restricted until 2082. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Box 105-108 (electronic media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 109-117 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Glen Horowitz Bookseller, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2018.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into seven series: I. Writings, 1974–2017. II. Correspondence, 1962–2017. III. Notebooks and Journals, 1960–2015. IV. Personal and Professional Papers, 1958–2017. V. Hanging Files, 1977–2017. VI. Printed Material, 1976–2016. VII. Computer and Audiovisual Media, 1979–2005.

bioghist.head: Eileen Myles (1949-)
bioghist.p: Eileen Myles is an American writer, educator, and performance artist who has published over twenty books of poetry, fiction, and nonfiction, including the autobiographical novel Chelsea Girls (1994). They were born on December 9, 1949 in Cambridge, Massachusetts, and graduated from the University of Massachusetts at Boston in 1971 with a B.A. in English. After moving to New York City in 1974, they studied in workshops at St. Mark's Poetry Project with Ted Berrigan, Alice Notley, and Paul Violi. They later served as the organization's artistic director from 1984 to 1986.

Myles is the author of books of poetry such as I Must Be Living Twice: New and Selected Poems 1974-2014 (2015) and Evolution (2018), the novel Cool for You (2000), and Afterglow (a dog memoir) (2017). Their poetry, essays, and articles have been featured in numerous anthologies and publications such as Harper's Magazine , The New Yorker , and The Village Voice . They additionally have appeared in film and television, and created performance pieces including The Collection of Silence for the Dia Center for the Arts in New York in 2010. Myles has taught writing and literature at Columbia University, New York University, University of California, San Diego, Bard College, and other institutions. Their awards include the American Academy of Arts and Letters Award, the Poetry Society of America Shelly Award, and four Lambda Book Awards. Myles has also received fellowships and grants from the Guggenheim Foundation, the National Endowment for the Arts, the MacDowell Colony, and Creative Capital.
Myles lives in New York City and Marfa, Texas. They use they/them pronouns.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Eileen Myles Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Unusual or non-descriptive titles provided by creator have been retained. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing. Information in brackets is also supplied by staff.
Material formerly in Folder 781 was moved to Box 61, Folder 1525a. Material formerly in Folder 782 were separated from the collection and returned to the creator of the material.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains writings, correspondence, notebooks and journals, personal and professional papers, printed material, computer and audiovisual media, and other papers by or relating to the writer, poet, and performance artist Eileen Myles. Writings consist of notes and drafts of Myles's books, poetry, plays, performance art, articles, essays, and other works. Correspondence includes exchanges with other writers, poets, editors, and artists in the United States and abroad, particularly the second generation of the New York School of poets. Correspondents include Dodie Bellamy, Charles Bernstein, Ted Berrigan, Tom Carey, C. A. Conrad, Dennis Cooper, Robert Creeley, Tory Dent, Tim Dlugos, Nicole Eisenman, Cliff Fyman, Barbara Guest, Nathan Kernan, Kevin Killian, Ali Liebegott, Bernadette Mayer, Barbara McKay, Thurston Moore, Elinor Nauen, Alice Notley, Kevin Opstedal, Kay Rosen, Thomas Savage, Leslie Scalapino, James Schuyler, Mark So, Joey Soloway, Juliana Spahr, Michelle Tea, David Trinidad, Paul Violi, Anne Waldman, Joe Westmoreland, and Bill Zavatsky. Personal and professional papers consist of photographs, teaching files, presidential campaign files, and subject files. Hanging files contain a mix of correspondence, writings, notebooks, personal papers, and other materials. Also present in the collection are poetry and prose by other authors such as Stanya Kahn and Akilah Oliver.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Printed material received with the collection was removed for separate cataloging and can be accessed by searching the library's online catalog.
Cassava root formering in Box 29, Folder 847, and 2 bags containing food items formerly in Box 92, were discarded after digitization.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Eileen Myles Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings consist of drafts, notes, correspondence, and other materials related to books, poetry, performance art, plays, articles, essays, speeches, interviews, and various projects by Eileen Myles. The series also contains works by other writers and poets.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: Books, Poetry, Shorter Works, and Writings of Others.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Writings can also be found in Series IV. Personal and Professional Papers, and Series V. Hanging Files.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of notes, research materials, drafts, proofs, publicity and press clippings, reviews, and correspondence related to seventeen of Myles's published and unpublished books of poetry, fiction, and non-fiction, as well as books they edited. Books include Afterglow , Chelsea Girls , Cool for You , Inferno (a poet's novel) , Snowflake/different streets , and the unpublished novel "My Filmmaker."

did.unittitle: "Just Before and Just After," draft fragments
"My Dog (a memoir)" (working title), proposal, draft fragments, and notes
Nelly Reivdan notes and draft fragment

title.part: A Fresh Young Voice From the Plains
I Must Be Living Twice: new & selected poems, 1975-2014

did.unittitle: "To me, I mean: The selected poems of Eileen Myles" (working title), draft
"To me, I mean: The selected poems of Eileen Myles" (working title), draft
"To me, I mean: The selected poems of Eileen Myles" (working title), draft, corrected
"Drops: A proposal to the Ferdinand Foundation," draft fragments
"Drops: A proposal to the Ferdinand Foundations," draft fragments
"Drops: A proposal to the Ferdinand Foundation," draft fragment
"Drops: A proposal to the Ferdinand Foundation," draft fragment
"Drops: A proposal to the Ferdinand Foundation," draft fragment
[When you come to the city], draft fragment
Maxfield Parrish: early & new poems , reviews and publicity

title.part: The New Fuck You: Adventures in Lesbian Reading

did.unittitle: on my way , draft and book cover
"Sky's the Limit," panel and book covers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains notes, drafts, broadsides, and copies of Myles's published and unpublished poems from approximately 1974 to 2016. Materials include their early poems created in the Poetry Project at St. Mark's Church's workshops, and a collaboration with musician Thurston Moore.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title, with unsorted materials filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: [Give it to some other girl you chauvinistic pig]
How to write a lesbian poem
Poems for Eleven More Women Poets in the 21st Century

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of notes, drafts, clippings, and copies pertaining to articles, essays, interviews, performance art, plays, reviews, speeches, and other works written by Myles. Included are the performance art pieces Feeling Blue, Parts 1, 2 & 3 , Hell , and Modern Art , Myles's literary magazine Dodgems , and assorted notes and drafts of their poetry and prose.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title, with unsorted materials filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: "ACT UP New York: Activism, Art, and the AIDS Crisis"
"Afterword. The Scene: A Conversation between Dennis Cooper and Eileen Myles"
Barbara Guest talk, MSA Women's panel
"Don Draper, Lil Wayne, and the Economy of the 21st Century"
"Gay Rites: A Wedding in Denmark, A Ceremony in New York
Life, a performance by Eileen Myles , notebook
"Life and Times of the Human Hole"
"On the road (& at home) with Sister Spit"
"Our Love is Real, But We Are Not"
"Pistolary, the complete films of Peggy Ahwesh"
"The Ragpicker: A Walking Tour of the East Village"
"Today's Poets: Alice Notley and Ann Lauterbach at DIA"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of drafts and proofs of other writers' poetry, prose, plays, articles, and essays. Included are works by Dodie Bellamy, Charles Bernstein, C. A. Conrad, Tim Dlugos, Thomas Savage, Leslie Scalapino, James Schuyler, Mark So, David Trinidad, and Joe Westmoreland. Also present are interviews of Myles.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by author or title, with unsorted materials filed at the end of the sequence

did.unittitle: Dlugos, Tim, A Fast Life: The Collected Poems of Tim Dlugos , edited by David Trinidad
Mirage#4/Period(ical) , edited by Kevin Killian and Dodie Bellamy
Schuyler, James, Other Flowers: Uncollected Poems

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence consists of letters and printed email exchanges with writers, poets, artists, editors, literary agents, publishers, students, family, and friends. Correspondents include writers Dodie Bellamy, Charles Bernstein, Ted Berrigan, Tom Carey, C. A. Conrad, Dennis Cooper, Robert Creeley, Tory Dent, Tim Dlugos, Cliff Fyman, Barbara Guest, Nathan Kernan, Kevin Killian, Ali Liebegott, Bernadette Mayer, Barbara McKay, Elinor Nauen, Alice Notley, Kevin Opstedal, Thomas Savage, Leslie Scalapino, James Schuyler, Juliana Spahr, Michelle Tea, David Trinidad, Paul Violi, Anne Waldman, Joe Westmoreland, and Bill Zavatsky; artists Nicole Eisenman and Kay Rosen; composer Mark So; musician Thurston Moore; and screenwriter Joey Soloway.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Personal Correspondence, and Professional Correspondence.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Correspondence can also be found in Series IV. Personal and Professional Papers, and Series V. Hanging Files.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains incoming and outgoing correspondence. Also included are works shared by correspondents.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent, with unsorted correspondence filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: Hannibal, Genevieve, letters from Eileen Myles
Hannibal, Genevieve, letters to Eileen Myles
Myles, Ann "Nancy," letters from Eileen Myles
Myles, Ann "Nancy," letters to Eileen Myles
First Name Only, Candice, with carved cassava root

descriptivenote.p: First Name Only, Candice, with carved cassava root

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Carved cassava root discarded after digitization in 2024.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The material is restricted to research until January 1, 2082.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Professional correspondence contains material pertaining to Myles's career as a writer, performance artist, and educator, organized by publication, publisher, organization, or subject. Subjects include publishing and performance contracts, grants, and royalties.

did.unittitle: Letters of recommendation by Eileen Myles
Letters of recommendation for Eileen Myles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebooks and Journals feature Eileen Myles's diary entries, notes, and assorted early drafts of poetry and prose, dating from approximately 1960 to 2015. Also present are high school and college class notebooks.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in chronological order, with undated materials filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: Blank notebook inscribed by Laurie Weeks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of personal papers, teaching files, presidential campaign files, and subject files belonging to Eileen Myles.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: Personal Papers, Teaching Files, Presidential Campaign Files, and Subject Files. Each is arranged alphabetically by folder title, with unsorted materials filed at the end of the sequence.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 61 (student records): Restricted until 2082. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Personal and Professional Papers can also be found in Series V. Hanging Files.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal papers include address books, appointment books, artwork by others, clippings, student records, event posters and ephemera, photographs, press files, financial documents, and other papers. There are materials pertaining to Myles's dog Rosie, including a leash in keepsake box, as well as objects such as a rolodex, hats, jewelry, textiles, pin-back buttons, and rubber stamps.

did.unittitle: New York Queer Experimental Film Festival
Outfest 23rd Los Angeles Gay and Lesbian Film Festival
Poets and Scholars Symposium, Texas Institute
San Francisco International Lesbian and Gay Film Festival
I'm unprivileging [correspondence, photographs, and printed material for "A Secret Location on the Lower East Side" exhibit]
I'm unprivileging [correspondence, photographs, and printed material for "A Secret Location on the Lower East Side" exhibit]
Reviews and clippings organized by publication
Valuable books continued [correspondence, photographs, and printed material]
Jewelry boxes, with jewelry and European coins
Paige Gratland gift, including dried jerky and loose tea leaves

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Two bags containing tea leaves and beef jerky were discarded after digitization in 2024.

did.unittitle: Piggy bank, rubber duck, and beads
Transparent : A Multidisciplinary Symposium tote bag
Sister Spit's Ramblin' Road Show "U.S. Tour '97" t-shirt
Sister Spit's Ramblin' Road Show "State to State in '98" t-shirt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Teaching files contain coursework, syllabi, class notes, graded and ungraded student works, correspondence, teaching materials, and other papers related to Myles's teaching career at Bard College, Columbia University, Naropa Institute, New York University, University of California San Diego, and other programs. Also present are materials from writing workshops hosted by Myles.

did.unittitle: Fine Arts Work Center Summer Program
Master Class in Poetry student work [ungraded]
Otis College of Art and Design
Unsorted teaching materials and ungraded student work
Vermont Studio Center visual telephone game
Unsorted teaching materials and ungraded student work
Restricted: University of California San Diego graded student work

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 61 (student records): Restricted until 2082. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Presidential campaign files document Myles's write-in candidate campaign in the 1992 U.S. presidential election. Materials include correspondence, campaign mailings, ephemera, speeches, and press, as well as handmade campaign buttons.

did.unittitle: State of New York Election Law

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Subject files include clippings, notes, reference materials, grant applications, and other materials related to Myles's creative endeavors. Materials reflect their involvement with the Poetry Project at St. Mark's Church and the television show Transparent . Also present are a small amount of drafts and photocopies of Myles's writings, as well as correspondence.

did.unittitle: The Cambridge Literary Review Colouring Book
Documenting Lesbian Lives oral history project
The Poetry Project Board of Directors notes
So to Speak poetry contest judging

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of assorted writings, personal and professional correspondence, notebooks, personal papers, presidential campaign files, teaching files, and subject files, as compiled by Eileen Myles. Writings present in this series include drafts, notes, and photocopies of Myles's poems, articles, essays, and speeches, as well as writing by others. Also included are clippings, reviews, and interviews.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Journalism, art reviews, book reviews, columns

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed Material consists of chapbooks, zines, pamphlets, periodicals, little magazines, anthologies, newsletters, and other publications featuring the writings of Eileen Myles and other authors and artists. The majority of publications in this series are handmade, self-published, or printed by small presses. Myles's poetry and prose appears in many of the periodicals and anthologies. Their standalone works include the mimeograph poetry books The Irony of the Leash (1978) and Polar Ode (1979), a collaboration with Anne Waldman; the zine Mrs. Rejector (circa 1992); a chapbook of The Inferno (2003); and two issues of their literary magazine, Dodgems (1977–1979). Other writers, poets, and editors present include Dodie Bellamy, Charles Bernstein, Ted Berrigan, C. A. Conrad, Robert Creeley, Tim Dlugos, Cliff Fyman, Kevin Killian, Ali Liebegott, Bernadette Mayer, Elinor Nauen, Alice Notley, Akilah Oliver, Kevin Opstedal, Thomas Savage, Juliana Spahr, Michelle Tea, David Trinidad, Joe Westmoreland, and Bill Zavatsky.

Small press literary and poetry magazines in the series include Ahnoi , Belladonna , Caveman , Ecstatic Peace , Gandhabba , June Caprice , Little Light , Mag City , Mirage #3 "The Women's Issue," New Leaves Review , Out There , Pome , Rocky Ledge , Roof , Telephone , Scarlet , United Artists , and The World . Several works are associated with the Poetry Project at St. Mark's Church, including Life Without Parole (1976), an anthology of poems from Bill Zavatsky's workshop.

Additionally, the series includes a number of LGBTQ publications such as Dear World: Queer Art and Lit , Dork Dyke , Girljock , Hissy Fit , LTTR , and Ridykeulous . Also present are exhibition catalogs featuring essays by Myles and other writers, chapbooks of performance texts by artist Stanya Kahn, and a 1977 newsprint pamphlet of SCUM Manifesto by Valerie Solanas.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains computer and audiovisual media including compact discs, DVDs, floppy disks, audiocassettes, and videocassettes.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Computer Media, and Audiovisual Media.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 105-108 (electronic media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 109-117 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Myles's born digital files on compact discs, DVDs, floppy disks, and Digital Audio Tapes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes audio and video recordings of Myles's readings, performances, interviews, and other public events, as well as their 1992 presidential campaign. Also present are mixtapes, commercial sound recordings, and copies of films.

did.unittitle: Mark So correspondence, "Thought Nobody Has Read It", "You Knew There Was a Script", "The End of New England", Eileen Myles; Readings 44, Los Angeles
did.materialspec: The program is distorted at times. Audio, at times, sounds hollow or unclear. High background noise is present. Static and dropouts are present. Speed fluctuations are present.
